title: RV8GR virtual timing and bus test helpers
purpose: Provide reusable virtual observation helpers for RV8GR circuit tests without replacing real chip behavior.

chips.role: Manual, random-push, and fixed-frequency clock profile source
Stable, one-shot, and preset pulse-train switch source
Seeded delay/noise injector between chip output and destination input
Expected output checker for virtual chip/circuit/system tests

behavior.clock_source: Generates declared test events only. It must not mask missing edge-trigger behavior in real chips.
behavior.switch: Generates stable on/off states, one-shot push/release pulses, one-shot on/off pulses, and deterministic preset pulse trains.
behavior.phase_probe: Records one-hot T0/T1/T2 phase observations and flags zero-hot or multi-hot states.
behavior.bus_monitor: Records active named drivers per bus and reports contention when more than one driver is active.
behavior.rc_parasitic: Estimates breadboard R/C delay for selected nets. It guides scope targets and timing margins, but it is not chip definition truth and cannot sign off hardware.
behavior.delay_noise: Injects seeded delay, jitter, and optional glitch stress between chip outputs and destination inputs.
behavior.output_assert: Checks the observed output against the expected value so a virtual test fails loudly.
behavior.boundary: Virtual helpers may observe and stimulate tests, but functional state transitions must still be proven by real DB component models when those models exist.

unsafe_states: phase probe sees no active phase
phase probe sees more than one active phase
bus monitor sees multiple active drivers
RC estimate treated as hardware signoff without scoped destination-pin measurement
delay/noise profile has no deterministic seed
output checker is missing from a generated chip behavior bench
switch preset emits the wrong number of one-shot edges
5 MHz treated as hardware-proven instead of functional simulation

guarantees: Clock profiles include manual push, random 100 pushes up to 500 ms, 50 kHz, 1 MHz, 2 MHz, and 5 MHz
Switch profiles include stable on/off, one-shot press/release, one-shot on/off, and 100 pulses at 10 ms interval
Random push profile has deterministic seed and one logical tick per push
Phase probe catches zero-hot and multi-hot phase states
Bus monitor catches IBUS and DBUS multi-driver contention
RCParasitic computes tau and 10%-to-90% settling estimates for clock/reset/bus nets
DelayNoise injects deterministic delay/noise profiles for inter-chip nets
OutputAssert maps expected outputs to fail-loudly checks
5 MHz is marked functional simulation only

audience: students ages 10-15, still useful for older learners
